1. **"The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of comfort and convenience, but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy."** (From his book "Strength to Love," 1963)

This quote highlights King's emphasis on the importance of standing up for what is right, even in the face of adversity. It encourages readers to consider their own values and principles, and to be willing to take a stand, even when it's difficult.

2. **"Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate; only love can do that."** (From his book "Where Do We Go From Here?", 1967)

This quote emphasizes the importance of perseverance in the face of adversity, and the need to focus on love and compassion rather than hate and anger. It encourages readers to seek out solutions that promote understanding and unity, rather than division and conflict.

3. **"We must accept finite disappointment, but we must never lose infinite hope."** (From his book "Where Do We Go From Here?", 1967)

This quote highlights King's emphasis on the importance of perseverance in the face of adversity. It encourages readers to accept that setbacks and disappointments are a natural part of life, but to never give up hope for a better future.

4. **"The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice."** (From a speech delivered at the National Cathedral in Washington, D.C., 1963)

This quote emphasizes King's faith in the ultimate triumph of justice and equality. It encourages readers to persevere in the face of adversity, knowing that the struggle for justice is ultimately worth it.

5. **"If you can't fly, then run. If you can't run, then walk. If you can't walk, then crawl. But whatever you do, you have to keep moving forward."** (From a speech delivered in Montgomery, Alabama, 1955)

This quote highlights King's emphasis on the importance of perseverance and determination. It encourages readers to keep moving forward, even when faced with obstacles and challenges.

6. **"Faith is taking the first step even when you don't see the whole staircase."** (From a speech delivered in Montgomery, Alabama, 1955)

This quote emphasizes King's emphasis on the importance of faith and trust in the face of uncertainty. It encourages readers to take the first step, even when they don't see the whole path ahead.

7. **"The time is always right to do what is right."** (From a speech delivered in Montgomery, Alabama, 1955)

This quote highlights King's emphasis on the importance of taking action and standing up for what is right, even when it's difficult. It encourages readers to consider their own values and principles, and to be willing to take a stand, even when it's unpopular.

These quotes capture the essence of King's message on perseverance and are sure to inspire and motivate readers. They can be used to illustrate key points and themes in your educational publication, and to provide a deeper understanding of King's legacy and impact.
